title: haplopappus fremontii subsp. wardii
intro: Haplopappus fremontii subsp. wardii, commonly known as Ward's rabbitbrush or Fremont's rabbitbrush, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Haplopappus fremontii subsp. wardii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
